mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE increases exceed 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 20-day return in at least 50% of observed instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that depresses corporate earnings and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where INDPRO trades below its 12-month trend during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate squeeze that forces rapid portfolio de-risking and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% without a concurrent funds rate cut.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals a formal recession onset that severely impairs corporate cash flows and guarantees negative quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates supply-side recovery outpacing labor absorption, creating earnings momentum that drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during post-shock recovery periods over a 4-year window.
